---
title: "Summarize simulated data with duckdb"
subtitle: > 
  duckdb provides a high-performance to summarize massive data sets.
author: Kyle Baron
date: 2026-05-30
image: images/duckdb.png
categories: 
- duckdb
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Introduction
This is a quick blog post showing how you can summarize a very 
large set of simulations using duckdb. You can learn more about 
duckdb [here](https://duckdb.org/). The duckdb 
[R package](https://cran.r-project.org/package=duckdb) can take a long time to 
install, but will be worth it once your simulations grow out of control.

# Setup 

```{r}
#| message: false
library(mrgsim.ds)
library(DBI)
library(duckdb)
library(mirai)
library(dplyr)
library(arrow)
```

Set up a large simulation; we'll check the number of rows in the 
output once it finishes.

- 3000 subjects
- 2 dose levels
- 28 days of dosing
- Sample every hour

```{r}
mod <- modlib_ds("popex")

mod <- update(mod, outvars = "IPRED", end = 4*168, delta = 1)

data <- expand.ev(
  ID = 1:3000,
  amt = c(300, 1000), 
  ii = 24, 
  addl = 27
)

data <- mutate(data, dose = amt)
```

# Simulate

Simulate 1000 replicates.

```{r}
#| eval: false
unlink("temp-post", recursive = TRUE)

arrow::set_io_thread_count(2)

daemons(5, seed = 3579)

everywhere(library(mrgsim.ds))

out <- mirai_map(
  1:1000,
  \(x, mod, data) {
    mrgsim_ds(mod, data, tags = list(rep = x), recover = "dose")
  },
  .args = list(mod = mod, data = data)
)[.]

daemons(0)

out <- reduce_ds(out)

save_ds(out, "temp-post/duckdb.rds")
```

There are 4 billion rows in this simulation set across 1000 parquet files 
totaling 31 Gb of data on disk. It is important to note that the data are
stored in highly-compressed parquet files; it's only the simulation output 
object which provides access to tat data that is stored in `.rds` format. 
So the `.rds` file is very light. 

```{r}
fs::file_size("temp-post/duckdb.rds")
```

```{r}
sims <- read_ds("temp-post/duckdb.rds")

sims
```

Note that all your simulated data is now safely stored on disk. If your R 
session crashes or restarts for whatever reason, you can easily restart and 
pick up right where you left off without having to re-run the expensive 
simulation step.

# Summarize using duckdb

Summarize using duckdb; we'll take median, 5th and 95th percentiles of the 
Week 4 trough concentration by dose and replicate. 


```{r}
con <- dbConnect(duckdb())

ds <- as_arrow_ds(sims)

duckdb_register_arrow(con, "sims", ds)

system.time(
  
  summ <- dbGetQuery(con, "
    SELECT
      dose,
      time,
      rep, 
      COUNT(*)                     AS n,
      QUANTILE_CONT(IPRED, 0.025)  AS Lower,
      QUANTILE_CONT(IPRED, 0.5)    AS Median,
      QUANTILE_CONT(IPRED, 0.975)  AS Upper
    FROM sims
    WHERE time = 672
    GROUP BY time, dose, rep
    ORDER BY time, dose, rep
  ")

)

dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)
```

```{r}
as_tibble(summ)
```

## Summarize using duckdb pipeline

To do this, we work in the Apache Arrow R package and convert the arrow data 
set to a duckdb object first, without copying any data.

```{r}
library(arrow)

system.time({ 

summ2 <- 
  ds %>% 
  to_duckdb() %>% 
  filter(time == 672) %>% 
  group_by(dose, time, rep) %>% 
  summarise(
    n = n(), 
    Lower = quantile(IPRED, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Median = median(IPRED, na.rm = TRUE), 
    Upper = quantile(IPRED, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE), 
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>% collect()
  
})

summ2
```
